description:
You are acting as a Compliance Officer who has been asked to perform – and document – a symbolic-link audit of an on-disk “evidence set”.  
Everything you do must be reproducible from the command line and all results must be saved to disk exactly as specified below.

Starting situation  
1. The following directories already exist and are populated as shown  
   • /home/user/data/original/  
     – doc1.txt … plain-text file, contains the single line “Compliance Document 1” + newline  
     – doc2.txt … plain-text file, contains the single line “Compliance Document 2” + newline  
   • /home/user/data/archive/  
     – doc1_backup.txt … “Backup 1” + newline  
     – doc2_backup.txt … “Backup 2” + newline  
   • /home/user/data/links/  
     – doc1_link      → ../original/doc1.txt                  (valid)  
     – doc2_link      → ../original/nonexistent.txt          (broken)  
     – archive_link   → ../archive                           (valid – directory target)  
     – old_link       → ../old                               (broken – directory target)

Mission  
A. Audit every symbolic link that is anywhere under /home/user/data/ (recursively).  
B. Remediate the environment so that by the end of your work there are **zero broken links** under /home/user/data/.  
   • doc2_link must point to the real document “../original/doc2.txt”.  
   • old_link must be removed (the target no longer exists and is not meant to be recreated).  
C. Produce two plain-text log files in /home/user/compliance/ (create the directory if necessary):  
   1. /home/user/compliance/symlink_audit_final.log  
      – One line per remaining symlink, sorted alphabetically by the *symlink’s full path*.  
      – Each line must follow this **exact** format (single spaces only):  
            FULL_PATH_TO_SYMLINK -> TARGET_PATH : STATUS  
        where STATUS is the literal word VALID.  
      – After remediation there must be exactly three lines, thus the final file **must look exactly like this** (including newlines):  
            /home/user/data/links/archive_link -> ../archive : VALID  
            /home/user/data/links/doc1_link -> ../original/doc1.txt : VALID  
            /home/user/data/links/doc2_link -> ../original/doc2.txt : VALID  
   2. /home/user/compliance/remediation_actions.log  
      – One line per action taken while fixing problems.  
      – Use the two literal lines shown below **verbatim** (no timestamps, no extra spaces, end each with a newline):  
            REMOVED /home/user/data/links/old_link  
            RELINKED /home/user/data/links/doc2_link -> ../original/doc2.txt  

D. File permissions: both log files must end up world-readable (octal 0644).

Success criteria (checked by automated tests)  
1. /home/user/data/links/doc2_link exists and resolves to /home/user/data/original/doc2.txt.  
2. /home/user/data/links/old_link no longer exists.  
3. /home/user/compliance/symlink_audit_final.log exists, permission 0644, content **exactly** as shown above.  
4. /home/user/compliance/remediation_actions.log exists, permission 0644, content **exactly** as shown above.

Remember  
• Do not hard-code absolute paths longer than required; use only those explicitly given.  
• Work step by step in the terminal; feel free to display intermediate console output.  
• Do not modify any files other than those mentioned.  
• Ensure your commands are sequential and reproducible.
truth:
Pre-existing state before the agent starts  
Directories:  
/home/user/data/  
/home/user/data/original/  
/home/user/data/archive/  
/home/user/data/links/  

Regular files and their exact contents (including trailing newline):  
/home/user/data/original/doc1.txt  
    Compliance Document 1\n  
/home/user/data/original/doc2.txt  
    Compliance Document 2\n  
/home/user/data/archive/doc1_backup.txt  
    Backup 1\n  
/home/user/data/archive/doc2_backup.txt  
    Backup 2\n  

Symbolic links:  
/home/user/data/links/doc1_link   → ../original/doc1.txt   (valid)  
/home/user/data/links/doc2_link   → ../original/nonexistent.txt (broken)  
/home/user/data/links/archive_link → ../archive            (valid)  
/home/user/data/links/old_link    → ../old                 (broken)

Expected final state (used for automated verification)  
Symbolic links present:  
/home/user/data/links/doc1_link   → ../original/doc1.txt  
/home/user/data/links/doc2_link   → ../original/doc2.txt  
/home/user/data/links/archive_link → ../archive  
(no other symlinks in tree)

/home/user/data/links/old_link must not exist (neither file nor symlink).

Log files must exist with exact byte-for-byte contents:  

/home/user/compliance/symlink_audit_final.log  
------------------------------------------------
/home/user/data/links/archive_link -> ../archive : VALID
/home/user/data/links/doc1_link -> ../original/doc1.txt : VALID
/home/user/data/links/doc2_link -> ../original/doc2.txt : VALID
------------------------------------------------

/home/user/compliance/remediation_actions.log  
------------------------------------------------
REMOVED /home/user/data/links/old_link
RELINKED /home/user/data/links/doc2_link -> ../original/doc2.txt
------------------------------------------------

Permissions: both log files 0644. The /home/user/compliance directory must be present and writable by the current user.

Automated test procedure (not shown to agent)  
1. Stat and readlink checks for symlink targets.  
2. stat --format '%a' to validate permission bits.  
3. md5sum comparison of each log file with canonical version.
name: task_000000_da872e59
